jobs.description:
Are you interested in building next generation services that will re-define international trade at Amazon? Do you want to improve how shoppers around the world discover and purchase global merchandise? Amazon Exports and Expansion is building new services responsible for enhancing the international shopping experience. Global Store (GS) and Core Exports (CE), together, enable cross-border (XB) shopping for Amazon customers worldwide. CE serves cross-border customers in 200+ destinations and shopping on 11 Exports-enabled stores by providing access to 177M+ products at competitive prices, with a localized shopping experience, and a reliable and accurate delivery experience. GS complements the local flywheel in 14 countries across 59 source-destination pairs (arcs), creating an integrated experience, enabling customers to shop Amazon’s international selection on their local Amazon or 3P website. GS abstracts XB fulfillment processes to make it seamless for customers to shop for Amazon’s international selection while rendering a fully localized experience with access to competitive pricing in local currency, cross border DEX, C-returns, exclusive deals and promotions, local payment methods and local customer service.  DESCRIPTION: AEE Selection Tech team is seeking an experienced Software Development Engineer interested in disrupting the way products are made available to customer, sold, and distributed on a global scale. The right candidate will have hands-on involvement in building new tools, services, and distribution structures across Amazon’s growing footprint. The products and capabilities enabled by this role will deliver unprecedented selection and prices across Amazon’s 100MM+ customers, while innovating beyond established cross border norms. The right candidate will thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment with interactions across a wide variety of e-commerce topics, and will demonstrate competence in multiple fields and skill sets.  At Amazon, we are working to be the most customer-centric company on earth and enable products that meet our customers needs and demands while providing a safe experience throughout. To reach there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, dynamic, and dedicated individuals. Are you excited to help Amazon grow product selection across the globe?    Mentorship & Career Growth: Our team is dedicated to supporting new team members. Our team has a broad mix of experience levels and Amazon tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. Our senior engineers truly enjoy mentoring more junior engineers and engineers from non-traditional backgrounds through one-on-one mentoring and thorough, but kind, code reviews. We care about your career growth. We try to assign projects and tasks based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded engineer and enable them to take on more complex tasks in the future.   

Key job responsibilities
• Solve complex problems through data, rapidly.
• Design and develop state of the art software systems that address these complex and ambiguous problems.
• Own the inputs and the business outcome through cultivating a culture of relentless auditing and metric monitoring, automatically where possible.
This position which is ideal for candidates who are looking to deal with scaling challenges at Amazon and ready to build mission critical system software applications and tools. It is also an opportunity for freelance candidates to become domain experts and have an enormous opportunity to make a large impact on the design, architecture and development of consumer products. Depending on individual performance this experience can lead to permanent positions as Software Development Engineers at Amazon.

A day in the life
A day in the life As a Software Development Engineer on the team, you will take ownership over the software design, documentation, development, engineering approach, delivery, and support of systems built natively in AWS. In this role you will collaborate with leaders, work backward from customers, identify problems, propose innovative solutions, relentlessly raise standards, and have a huge impact by disrupting the way products are bought, sold, and distributed on Amazon on a global scale. The right candidate will have hands-on involvement in building new tools, services, and distribution structures across Amazon’s growing footprint. The products and capabilities enabled by this role will deliver unprecedented selection for our global customers while complying with Global Trade and Product Compliance requirements. The right candidate will thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment with interactions across a wide variety of e-commerce topics, and will demonstrate competence in multiple fields and skill sets.

jobs.description:
Annapurna Labs (our organization within AWS UC) designs silicon and software that accelerates innovation. Customers choose us to create cloud solutions that solve challenges that were unimaginable a short time ago—even yesterday. Our custom chips, accelerators, and software stacks enable us to take on technical challenges that have never been seen before, and deliver results that help our customers change the world.

Diverse Experiences
We value diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Inclusive Team Culture
It’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead and grow a team of SOC integration engineers responsible for critical deliverables in our ML accelerator chips
- Drive technical decisions across multiple disciplines (RTL, timing, DFT, physical design)
- Ensure on-time delivery of complex SOC integration milestones
- Establish and maintain best practices for top-level integration, including clock/reset architecture, CDC methodology, and quality metrics
- Interface with various stakeholders including architecture, verification, and physical design teams
You will thrive in this role if you:
- Have a proven track record leading SOC integration teams through successful tape-outs
- Excel at cross-functional leadership and can drive complex technical decisions across multiple teams
- Are passionate about developing engineers and implementing robust integration methodologies
- Have deep expertise in high-performance SOC design, including clock/reset architecture, timing closure, and CDC methodology
- Are data-driven and can effectively balance technical excellence with project execution

About the team
Custom SoCs (System on Chip) live at the heart of AWS Machine Learning servers. As a member of the Cloud-Scale Machine Learning Acceleration team you’ll be responsible for the design and optimization of hardware in our data centers including AWS Inferentia, our custom designed machine learning inference datacenter server. Our success depends on our world-class server infrastructure; we’re handling massive scale and rapid integration of emergent technologies. We’re looking for an ASIC Design Eengineer to help us trail-blaze new technologies and architectures, while ensuring high design quality and making the right trade-offs.

Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge-sharing and mentorship. Our senior members enjoy one-on-one mentoring and thorough, but kind, code reviews. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects that help our team members develop your engineering expertise so you feel empowered to take on more complex tasks in the future.

jobs.description:
At AWS Professional Services (ProServe), we build customer solutions that accelerate business outcomes and reinvent customer and employee experiences. The Generative AI Experience Center Solutions Specialist role creates industry-grade demonstrations that provide AWS customers with real-time buying capability in a physical space.

You will educate customers and partners on AI technologies transforming industries—financial services, public sector, and healthcare across automated reasoning, physical AI digital solutions, regulatory compliance, and advanced analytics. You will build high-quality demonstrations, provide technical guidance to activate solutions, and offer hands-on architectural guidance for customer projects involving AWS services and ISV/partner solutions.

As the primary guide for customers visiting the GenAI Experience Center, you will deliver compelling tours that translate technical capabilities into business value. You will present demonstrations in one-on-one and group settings, adapting your approach based on customer industry, technical background, and business objectives. You will facilitate hands-on experiences with our rotating showcase of pan-Amazon innovations and partner solutions.

You'll collaborate with cross-Amazon teams to develop outcome-based solutions for strategic opportunities and work with partner teams to maintain demonstration readiness and gather customer feedback. Your insights will inform future demonstration development and identify follow-up engagement opportunities.

In this role, you'll serve as demo creator, storyteller, presenter, and customer advocate creating memorable experiences that drive meaningful conversations about AI transformation and generate pipeline opportunities for AWS and our partners.

Key job responsibilities
• Deliver engaging tours of the GenAI Experience Center for customers and partners
• Adapt presentations based on audience industry, role, and technical expertise
• Facilitate hands-on demonstrations and interactive experiences
• Gather customer feedback and insights to improve the visitor experience
• Coordinate with internal teams to ensure demonstration readiness
• Support pipeline tracking and follow-up engagement coordination


About the team
ABOUT AWS:
 
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
 
Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. 

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. You’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Generation Service Technician has safety, customer satisfaction, and business responsibilities within data center operations. The person in this role is responsible for identifying, troubleshooting, diagnosing and providing quality repairs of electrical generator systems and related engine mechanical failures. As both a technical expert and primary customer support liaison, the Generator Service Technician is responsible for maintaining consistent communication with clients to ensure an exceptional customer experience while maximizing data center availability. All necessary tools and equipment will be provided by AWS.

Key job responsibilities
- Know and follow all Company safety rules, including maintaining a safe, clean, and orderly work area, and fully participate in company accident prevention and safety improvement activities
- Respond to customer requests
- Travel to designated sites and perform professional quality field service repairs consistent with company standards
- Advise the Generator Service Manager of parts needed for necessary repairs
- Keep Generator Service Manager continually apprised, and utilize leadership to assist in answering customer questions and problems when necessary
- Document detailed, complete and legible descriptions of all repair operations performed
- Accurately record time, expenses, and mileage
- Travel 80-90% of the time in an assigned region, and/or work overtime, weekends, or holidays, sometimes on short notice
- Lift up to 40 lbs frequently; pushing and pulling a maximum force of 40 lbs
- Wear Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Protective Glasses, Protective Gloves, and Protective Footwear as required

A day in the life
The Generator Service Technician has safety, customer satisfaction, and business responsibilities within data center operations. The person in this role is responsible for identifying, troubleshooting, diagnosing and providing quality repairs of electrical generator systems and related engine mechanical failures.

The Generator Service Technician is both the technical expert and primary customer support contact to the customer, and is therefore responsible for continually communicating with the customer to provide an exceptional customer experience and drive data center up-time and availability.

jobs.description:
External job description
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Operations Manager. In this role, you will lead and develop a team of salaried and hourly talent. You will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. To achieve this, managers are expected to provide their team with the tools needed for success while driving improvements in productivity and efficiency through data-driven decisions and analytical problem-solving. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Operations Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.

Key job responsibilities
Key Responsibilities and Job Elements:

-Support, mentor, and motivate your salaried and hourly workforce

-Lead large-scope projects with site and regional impact

-Build and execute productivity plans through forecast reviews, determining productivity requirements, and partnering with other Leaders to load balance

-Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises

-Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives

-Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach

-Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts

-Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud

-Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)

-Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)

jobs.description:
Il ruolo dell’IT Technician nei team Operations di Amazon è assicurare la piena operatività dei vari sistemi IT che rappresentano il cuore pulsante delle nostre sedi. Come IT Support Technician, lavorerai presso un centro logistico in sinergia con i tuoi colleghi, gli addetti all’assistenza remota e le funzioni commerciali, assicurando un supporto fondamentale in ambito IT. Avrai l’opportunità di lavorare con le tecnologie più avanzate del settore, in un contesto lavorativo che promuove la crescita personale e professionale attraverso attività di affiancamento. Facendo leva sulle risorse di formazione che ti verranno offerte dal tuo team, potrai sviluppare la tua carriera lavorativa, ampliare le tue competenze tecniche secondo i tuoi ritmi e ottenere qualifiche riconosciute dall’intero settore.

Key job responsibilities
Assistere utenti in loco fornendo supporto in attività di installazione, assistenza e risoluzione di problemi relativi all’infrastruttura IT, ai dispositivi e alle applicazioni per gli utenti finali.

Gestire le richieste di assistenza, fornendo supporto come primo e secondo punto di contatto con i clienti.

Fornire una manutenzione preventiva per massimizzare la disponibilità di tutte le attrezzature informatiche.

Attuare progetti e creare innovazione insieme ad altri colleghi dell’assistenza IT.

A day in the life
Fin dal primo giorno di lavoro potrai contribuire a migliorare la nostra produttività, implementando le tecnologie più innovative e aiutando i colleghi a sfruttare al meglio le attrezzature IT. Se cerchi un ambiente di lavoro estremamente dinamico, questa è la tua occasione per metterti in gioco. Potrai suggerire nuovi metodi per migliorare i nostri sistemi e collaborerai con i tuoi colleghi per ottimizzare e affinare, ove possibile, l’assistenza offerta ai clienti interni.

About the team
Il team Ops Tech Solutions (OTS) offre supporto tecnico costante alle operazioni globali di Amazon. Per occuparsi della manutenzione, dell’upgrade e della protezione dell’infrastruttura informatica e dei dispositivi degli utenti finali di una delle aziende più grandi del mondo, il team possiede competenze varie. Il team vanta al suo interno sia ingegneri che aiutano ad implementare soluzioni e crearne di nuove, che figure gestionali che guidano team dell’assistenza tecnica per offrire un supporto ottimale agli utenti finali.
Il team OTS di Amazon non risparmia energie per conciliare prestazioni elevate e attenzione alla sostenibilità in tutte le nuove soluzioni adottate. Con molti membri che operano in loco e turni che coprono l’intera giornata, il team assicura un’assistenza continua ed effettua interventi di manutenzione importanti riducendo al minimo le interruzioni delle attività.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience in marketing, brand advertising, media sales, or online advertising and close work with senior advertisers and agency stakeholders
- Experience prioritizing and handling multiple assignments at any given time while maintaining commitment to deadlines, or experience in a fast-paced, high-tech company
- Experience with CRM tools like Salesforce
- Bachelor's degree in a relevant field or equivalent work experience
jobs.description:
Amazon Ads is dedicated to driving measurable outcomes for advertisers. Our ad solutions—including sponsored, display, video, and custom ads—leverage Amazon’s innovations and insights to find, attract, and engage intended audiences throughout their daily journeys. With a range of flexible pricing and industry leading tech, these solutions help advertisers build awareness, increase consideration and convert. We start with the customer and work backwards in everything we do, including advertising. If you’re interested in joining a rapidly growing team working to build a unique, world-class advertising group with a relentless focus on the customer, you’ve come to the right place.
Amazon Ads is seeking an Account Executive to join our newly formed Government & Advocacy Ads team within our Large Customer Sales organization. This is an exciting time to join a new team in a role with broad scope and the potential to make real impact. This role will be focused on driving net-new business with a prospecting mindset. As the first line of communication with prospective advertisers, ideal candidates will have a strong understanding of the sales process, excel at researching leads, nurturing new relationships, pitch presentations, proposal generations, and driving ad investment. The Government & Advocacy Account Executive will focus on delivering advertising revenue through strategic partnerships with government organizations, non-profits, advocacy groups, agencies and related organizations, helping them leverage Amazon Advertising solutions to reach their intended audiences.


Key job responsibilities
- Generate net-new advertising revenue through strategic selling to ad agencies, non-profits, government agencies and trade groups.
- Be a true hunter, hungry to uncover opportunity and connect it with a value adding solution.
- Proactively build and maintain relationships with key decision-makers in government, advocacy organizations, non-profits and their respective agencies.
- Create and deliver custom sales presentations and media plans that align Amazon's advertising solutions with advertiser campaign objectives.
- Develop consultative joint business plans. Thoughtfully respond to RFPs with effective solutions and tailored recommendations.
- Own customer conversations from agenda and attendees to final presentation.
- Earn customer trust through deep industry, landscape and Amazon Ad knowledge.
- Generate and maintain a robust pipeline of opportunities.
- Leverage existing industry relationships to identify new business and revenue opportunities for Amazon Ads.
- Anticipate and identify advertising customer needs. Align with internal stakeholders to overcome roadblocks and deliver bar-raising solutions.
- Provide superior customer service while maintaining compliance with advertising policies.
- Work cross-functionally with internal teams to ensure successful campaign set up, optimization, reporting and execution.

jobs.description:
We are seeking a skilled Rack Install Technician to join our Infrastructure Delivery team. The successful candidate will be responsible for installing and cabling server racks as well as fulfilling prerequisites for the Rack Install process.

Key job responsibilities
- Server rack installation

- Testing and verification of installed equipment

- Following detailed technical documentation and installation procedures

- Maintain accurate documentation of installations and modifications

- Follow safety protocols and data center security procedures

- Collaborate with team members and other technical staff

About the team
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.description:
Amazon Logistics is seeking an experienced Senior Program Manager to join the North America Last Mile Fleet Planning & Optimization team within the Global Fleet Products organization. In this role, you will drive end-to-end improvement of fleet utilization and reduce dependency on expensive third-party (3P) rental fleet — by monitoring execution of fleet allocation activities and measuring performance against weekly and monthly published fleet plans.
You will lead programs that systematically improve how Amazon's branded fleet is deployed, rebalanced, and utilized across the NA last mile network. You will be the connective tissue between fleet planning outputs and operational reality — translating weekly fleet plans into actionable allocations, monitoring execution across the network, and communicating progress, risks, and optimization opportunities to senior leadership on a regular cadence.


Key job responsibilities
• Fleet Utilization Optimization: Lead programs designed to improve branded fleet utilization across the last mile network — identifying underutilized assets, driving rebalancing actions, and reducing reliance on costly 3P rental fleet as a substitute for available branded capacity.
• Rental Fleet Reduction Programs: Monitor and manage initiatives aimed at reducing 3P rental fleet usage by ensuring branded fleet is fully deployed, rebalanced proactively, and available where needed. Track rental spend and utilization trends, and drive cross-functional actions to close gaps.
• Plan Execution & Monitoring: Track execution against published fleet plans on a continuous basis, identifying gaps, risks, and deviations early. Monitor whether rebalancing actions are being executed, whether vehicles are moving as planned.
• Stakeholder Coordination: Partner closely with Regional Fleet Managers (RFMs), DSPs, OTR teams, Tiger Teams, and Fleet Planning to align on allocation decisions and resolve blockers through structured weekly checkpoints and ad hoc escalations.
• Leadership Reporting: Develop and deliver clear, data-driven reporting and narratives to senior leadership on fleet allocation status, utilization performance, rental fleet trends, execution progress, risks, and mitigation actions.
• Program Governance: Establish and maintain program rhythms, mechanisms, and documentation to support consistent, scalable fleet allocation and optimization execution across programs including Deployment, Redeployment, LMR andVariable Fleet

jobs.company_name: Amazon Kuiper Services Europe SARL, UK Branch
jobs.description:
Join our team as an Account Executive focused on driving enterprise sales for Amazon Leo solutions across the DACH region. In this pivotal role, you will leverage your expertise in cloud networking and satellite communications to build strategic relationships with C-level executives at Europe's largest multinational and domestic corporations. You will be responsible for identifying opportunities, understanding complex connectivity needs, and proposing high-value network solutions worth millions of dollars. This position requires exceptional enterprise sales acumen, technical knowledge of connectivity solutions, and the ability to navigate complex contract negotiations with senior decision makers.

As Germany represents our largest potential market, you will play a crucial role in expanding our footprint in this key region, with additional responsibility for Austria and Switzerland. Your experience selling large-scale networking and connectivity solutions will be instrumental in helping customers transform their operations through Amazon's cutting-edge satellite technology. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and are passionate about building relationships with enterprise customers to deliver innovative solutions, this role offers the opportunity to make a significant impact on our European expansion strategy.

This position may require access to information, technology, or hardware that is subject to export control laws and regulations, including the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) and the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR). Employment in this position is contingent upon obtaining any required export licenses or other approvals from the United States government. As such, the successful candidate must be eligible to obtain any necessary export licenses or approvals based on their nationality, citizenship, and any other factors considered by the applicable export control regulations.

Key job responsibilities
- Drive revenue and increase market share across assigned enterprise accounts in Europe, with special focus on the German market, by developing and executing strategic sales plans for Amazon Leo solutions
- Build and maintain relationships with C-level executives at multinational corporations, becoming a trusted advisor who understands their complex connectivity challenges and business objectives
- Propose and negotiate multi-million dollar networking and satellite communication solutions that address customer needs, managing the full sales cycle from prospecting to contract closure
- Collaborate with internal technical teams to develop customized proposals and demonstrations that effectively communicate the value proposition of Amazon Leo to enterprise decision makers
- Maintain a robust sales pipeline and accurate forecasting while working with partners to extend reach and drive adoption of satellite connectivity solutions in key European markets

A day in the life
Your day begins with reviewing your pipeline and prioritizing outreach to enterprise prospects and existing customers. Throughout the day, you'll engage with C-level executives across the DACH regiona to understand their connectivity challenges and articulate how Amazon Leo solutions can transform their operations. You'll collaborate with technical teams to develop customized proposals, conduct demonstrations, and progress deals through the sales cycle. A significant portion of your time will be spent building relationships with German multinational companies, understanding their specific requirements, and negotiating contracts for large-scale connectivity solutions.

About the team
Our team is responsible for all direct sales to enterprise B2B customers across Europe. We work at the forefront of satellite communications technology, helping major corporations transform their connectivity capabilities through innovative Amazon Leo solutions. We are passionate about solving complex business challenges and pride ourselves on building long-term relationships with our customers.

As we expand our presence in strategic European markets, with particular focus on Germany as our largest potential market, we're looking for experienced sales professionals who can engage effectively with senior executives and drive significant growth. Our collaborative environment encourages innovative thinking and provides opportunities to work on high-impact projects that shape the future of enterprise connectivity solutions.
